title: [ci:docs]Update podman-image-exists.1.md
body:
Adds an example of using podman image exists in a script that also has errexit set.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md

```release-note
none
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *buckaroogeek* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign baude for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I am fine with this.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @edsantiago WDYT?

Change the example to

if podman image exists webbackend; then
    echo "webbackend exists"
...

And it would probably be helpful

I really have to push against this. This PR is spending almost twenty lines of valuable space (and, more importantly, user attention) to re-document what is already in the man page:

>A 1 will be returned otherwise.

Anyone who uses `set -e` should understand what that means.

I must once again insist that we not accept this PR.

> A simple example like this may save someone a few hours. 

The fallacy here is that yes, it might, but only a "someone" who matches a very tightly defined set of conditions:
* very new to UNIX shell programming; and
* writing a script with `-e` without understanding `-e`; and
* running `podman image exists` in a way that is counter to its documentation.

Granted, that set of users is greater than or equal to one. But if we accept this, we must also accept that same examples for `podman network exists`, and `podman volume exists`; and maybe for any command that exits nonzero; and maybe add some examples showing how asterisks in a command (ls, search) will surprise someone not familiar with shell globbing; and brackets; maybe angle brackets too; and by extension add tutorials to all our man pages. It would be the equivalent of adding, to a the air-conditioning section of a vehicle owner's manual, "if, while you're driving, you see a traffic light turn red, you should move your foot from the accelerator pedal to the brake pedal and depress it gently in such a manner as to bring the vehicle to a stop."

The unmentioned element is cost. Adding trivial examples will cost people time: anyone with basic shell scripting experience would read your example and think... okay... there must be a reason why the maintainers added all this verbiage for something that is obvious. What's the hidden catch? What am I missing? Someone may spend a long time looking at that, looking for a hidden subtlety where there is none.

We need to require some minimum proficiency from our target audience. Understanding shell exit status is essential to using podman. I feel confident that, in one or two weeks' time, you will have enough understanding of this to agree with me. 

This is in no way intended as a slight on you! Once again, your initiative in learning podman and shell scripting is praiseworthy, and I love seeing anyone venturing into new territory! So, seriously, thank you.
